question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `endpointOverride(sourceProperties.getUri())` call by poisoning the `uri` property in application configuration (e.g., via environment variable injection or misconfigured externalized config) to redirect all stream file downloads to an attacker-controlled server, causing the importer to ingest tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `URI.create(sourceProperties.getType().getEndpoint())` fallback to resolve to an attacker-controlled endpoint by manipulating the `SourceType` enum endpoint value through a misconfigured property source, redirecting stream downloads and allowing injection of forged Hashgraph record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `AnonymousCredentialsProvider.create()` path (triggered when `isAnonymousCredentials()` returns true) to access a publicly writable or misconfigured S3 bucket and upload tampered stream files that the importer will then download and process as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `DefaultCredentialsProvider.builder().build()` fallback path by injecting AWS credentials via environment variables (`AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`) in a shared or containerized environment, causing the importer to authenticate with attacker-controlled credentials and fetch tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `LOCAL` source type branch (`new LocalStreamFileProvider(...)`) by placing tampered stream files in the local filesystem path that the importer reads from, if the local path is world-writable or accessible via a shared volume, causing the importer to process forged Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `forcePathStyle(true)` setting combined with a manipulated `bucketName` property containing path traversal sequences (e.g., `../`) to redirect S3 object fetches to unintended bucket paths, allowing injection of tampered Hashgraph record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user perform an SSRF attack by supplying a `uri` value of `http://169.254.169.254/` (AWS metadata endpoint) via the `endpointOverride(sourceProperties.getUri())` call, causing the importer to leak IAM credentials that can then be used to upload tampered stream files to the legitimate bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation in the `NettyNioAsyncHttpClient` configuration to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on stream file downloads, substituting tampered Hashgraph record files in transit without the importer detecting the substitution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `isStaticCredentials()` logic (which always returns `true` for `SourceType.GCP`) to force the use of static credentials even when none are configured, causing a null pointer exception or fallback to empty credentials that grant access to a misconfigured GCS bucket containing Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the logging of `commonDownloaderProperties.getBucketName()` and `blockProperties.getBucketName()` in `streamFileProviders()` to enumerate bucket names from exposed log endpoints, then use those names to target the correct S3/GCS bucket for uploading tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Region.of(sourceProperties.getRegion())` call with an attacker-controlled region string to redirect S3 requests to a different AWS region where the attacker controls a bucket with the same name, causing the importer to download tampered Hashgraph stream files from the attacker's bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `connectionMaxIdleTime(Duration.ofSeconds(5))` setting to force frequent connection recycling, creating a race condition window during reconnection where a DNS rebinding attack can redirect the S3 client to an attacker-controlled server serving tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: awasCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `StaticCredentialsProvider.create(AwsBasicCredentials.create(credentials.getAccessKey(), credentials.getSecretKey()))` path by observing credentials leaked in logs or error messages, then using those credentials to upload tampered stream files to the Hashgraph history bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `commonDownloaderProperties.getPathPrefix()` value being logged when non-blank to infer the internal bucket path structure, then craft targeted uploads of tampered Hashgraph stream files to the exact path the importer will fetch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of hostname verification in the `NettyNioAsyncHttpClient` built by `s3Client()` to perform DNS spoofing against the S3 endpoint, redirecting stream file downloads to an attacker-controlled server that returns tampered Hashgraph record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `uri` with an `http://` scheme (instead of `https://`) via `endpointOverride(sourceProperties.getUri())` to downgrade the connection to plaintext, enabling interception and replacement of Hashgraph stream files in transit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the anonymous `ExecutionInterceptor` implementation in `overrideConfiguration()` that calls `modifyHttpRequest()` to inject additional query parameters by controlling `projectId`, causing GCS to serve files from an attacker-specified project and thereby injecting tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the iteration over `commonDownloaderProperties.getSources()` in `streamFileProviders()` to inject a malicious source entry (e.g., via a misconfigured config server) that points to an attacker-controlled endpoint, causing the importer to download tampered Hashgraph stream files from that source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `maxConcurrency(sourceProperties.getMaxConcurrency())` setting (default 1000) to launch a resource exhaustion attack that overwhelms the importer's HTTP connection pool, causing it to time out on legitimate stream file downloads and fall back to a cached or stale version of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `connectionTimeout(sourceProperties.getConnectionTimeout())` setting by causing the legitimate S3 endpoint to be slow/unreachable, forcing the importer to time out and retry against a fallback endpoint that the attacker controls, resulting in tampered Hashgraph stream files being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `DefaultCredentialsProvider` path by placing a crafted `~/.aws/credentials` file in the importer process's home directory (e.g., via a file upload vulnerability elsewhere in the system), causing the importer to authenticate with attacker-controlled AWS credentials and fetch tampered Hashgraph stream files?
